name: Gambling Advertising
description: Campaigns for sports betting, online casinos, lotteries, and other gambling products. Triggers jurisdiction-specific restrictions, self-exclusion compliance, responsible gambling messaging, and audience composition requirements beyond standard age-gating.

regulatory_frameworks.name: UK Gambling Commission
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Licence Conditions and Codes of Practice (LCCP). Prohibits targeting self-excluded individuals, requires responsible gambling messaging, restricts content that appeals to children or vulnerable persons.

regulatory_frameworks.name: US State Gambling Laws
regulatory_frameworks.summary: State-by-state gambling advertising regulations. Geo-fencing to legal jurisdictions, self-exclusion list compliance, responsible gambling messaging. Requirements vary from permissive (NJ, PA) to restrictive (UT, HI ban gambling entirely).

regulatory_frameworks.name: EU Member State Regulations
regulatory_frameworks.summary: Varies by member state. Italy bans all gambling advertising. Spain restricts to 1am-5am. Netherlands requires responsible gambling messaging and prohibits untargeted digital ads.

guidance: Gambling advertising has requirements distinct from general age-restricted products: self-exclusion list compliance (operators must not target individuals who have opted out), responsible gambling messaging, and jurisdiction-level legality (not just age). Health data is restricted because problem gambling indicators and addiction-related data must not be used for targeting. Governance agents must verify geo-targeting matches jurisdictions where the specific gambling product is legal.
